:source — biais déclaré :bias. Couvertur: :source — declared bias: :bias. Coverage archived by GrimbaNews.
<strong>Cadrage linguistique</strong> —: Linguistic framing — lexical analysis of headlines and standfirsts (vocabulary, connotations, semantic field), compared to a reference corpus.
<strong>Choix éditorial des sujets</stro: Editorial topic choice — which stories are covered, which are missing from the feed, over a rolling thirty-day window.
<strong>Fact-checking</strong> — taux d': Fact-checking — rate of corrected or retracted articles over the last twelve months.
<strong>Historique éditorial</strong> —: Editorial track record — line consistency, longevity, journalistic awards.
<strong>Indépendant</strong> — capital d: Independent — capital held by the newsroom or by shareholders without significant other economic activity.
<strong>Pluralisme</strong> — les angles: Pluralism — left, center, and right angles are always represented when they exist. When one is missing, we flag it (blindspot).
<strong>Privé</strong> — détenu par un g: Private — owned by an industrial or financial group ("interest-driven" press).
<strong>Révision continue</strong> — nos: Continuous review — our classifications evolve. An outlet can move up or down the grid based on its practices.
<strong>Sourcing</strong> — part d'artic: Sourcing — share of articles with explicit sources (names, documents, data).
<strong>Structure de l'argumentation</st: Argument structure — in editorials and op-eds, ratio between sources cited on the left, center, and right.
<strong>Séparation info / opinion</stron: News / opinion separation — clear distinction between wire copy, investigations, analyses, and editorials.
<strong>Transparence</strong> — chaque c: Transparency — every classification is public; every source has a profile showing its score, orientation, and ownership type.
<strong>Validation croisée externe</stro: External cross-validation — our classifications are calibrated against the AllSides and Media Bias/Fact Check methodologies, adapted to the Francophone media landscape.
<strong>État</strong> — financé par l'ar: State — funded by public money; newsroom autonomy varies by country.
Ad Fontes Media — analyse linguistique +: Ad Fontes Media — linguistic analysis + review committee

C'est la convention francophone et conti: That's the Francophone and continental convention. In the United States the convention is reversed — that's what creates the confusion on Anglo tools. We chose one stable color code, Francophone by default, for every edition of the site, and we document it on the "Understanding the barometer" page.

Calcul basé sur le biais individuel des: Calculated from the individual bias of articles archived from this source. When this bar diverges sharply from the declared editorial bias, it reveals unexpected angles in the coverage.

Chaque article ingéré par GrimbaNews est: Every article ingested by GrimbaNews is tagged with its original language (French or English). The detector combines four signals, in this order — first wins:

Concrètement, un cluster bascule en just: In practice, a cluster flips to middle ground when the left-count equals the right-count (or differs by one) AND neither side exceeds the center. The rule lives in GrimbaClusterBias::resolve() and is replayed nightly at 03:35 UTC over the last 555 articles. Tagged clusters carry the internal label 'mg_<L>_<C>_<R>' and appear in purple across the site — bias-legend chip, /comparatif?diversity=middle_ground tab, dedicated /juste-milieu page, /feed.juste-milieu.xml RSS feed.
Connectez-vous pour lire le texte intégr: Log in to read the full text extracted by GrimbaNews.
Création de campagne en libre-service, t: Self-serve campaign creation, real-time telemetry, Stripe billing, editorial-review queue. The advertiser platform launches in the coming days — leave us your email for early access.

Factualité estimée depuis le score de cr: Factuality estimated from each source credibility score.
Filtrer par fiabilité factuelle. Les pal: Filter by factual reliability. The tiers (Very high, High, Mixed, Low, Very low) are based on each source's historical fact-checking score — not the individual article.
Filtrez par camp : « Pour la gauche » =: Filter by camp: "For the left" = stories the right ignores, and vice-versa. Lets you see what you naturally avoid.
Gauche, Centre, Droite. Chaque source, c: Left, Center, Right. Every source, every article.
GrimbaNews calcule chaque jour les angle: GrimbaNews computes blindspots daily from the story clusters our engine groups. A cluster is flagged as a blindspot when coverage exceeds 80% from a single camp across at least six distinct sources.
GrimbaNews cible cette absence : un seul: GrimbaNews targets that absence: a single Francophone tool that aggregates what's written, labels each source's editorial bias and reliability, and clearly flags blindspots. Not another news feed — a reading frame.
GrimbaNews est un média francophone indé: GrimbaNews is an independent Francophone newsroom published by Iboga Ventures. Our goal: to let our readers see every angle of every story. We don't claim neutrality — we make it visible, quantifiable, and challengeable.
GrimbaNews est une plateforme francophon: GrimbaNews is a Francophone platform that surfaces editorial bias and the blindspots of the news.
GrimbaNews is published by Iboga Ventures. We accept no advertising funding from political parties, governments, or press groups whose bias we classify. Our revenue comes exclusively from subscriptions and non-profit sponsorship.
GrimbaNews utilise des cookies essentiel: GrimbaNews uses essential cookies for site operation (language preferences, reading mode, local reading history) and, with your consent, anonymized audience-measurement cookies. You can change your mind at any time from the privacy page.
GrimbaNews — votre vue panoramique sur l: GrimbaNews — your panoramic view on the news
Histoires couvertes de manière dispropor: Stories disproportionately covered by only one side of the political spectrum.
Histoires couvertes en proportions égale: Stories covered in equal proportions by the left and the right.
Histoires que gauche, centre et droite c: Stories the left, center, and right are covering at the same time
Impressions, clics, taux de complétion,: Impressions, clicks, completion rate, and engagement signal per campaign — CSV-exported weekly.
Impressions, vues uniques, CTR par empla: Impressions, unique views, CTR per slot, average reading time on the slot page, and video completion rate if applicable.
Indiquez où vous êtes pour obtenir l'act: Tell us where you are to get local news and relevant French or international sources.
La concentration éditoriale n'est pas un: Editorial concentration is not a detail. When the same group owns outlets with different biases, the apparent diversity of the media landscape hides a convergence of interests at the top.
La cote de chaque source est la moyenne: Each source's rating is the weighted average of these four signals. The raw score is viewable on each source's page.
La direction éditoriale est indépendante: The editorial leadership is independent of the executive leadership. No content can be pulled at a shareholder's request.
La lecture du site est gratuite — homepa: The site is free to read — homepage, source list, dossiers, blindspots, /coffre. The paid tier unlocks the full article text (instead of the excerpt + external link), removes ad slots, and unlocks the daily and thematic newsletters. Blindspots are NEVER paywalled.
La plupart des lecteurs francophones n'o: Most Francophone readers don't have access to a tool that shows, on the same story, what the left, center, and right are writing. Anglo aggregators exist but are biased by their US-centric reference grid. The Francophone press is what it is. The Francophone African press is under-represented everywhere.
Le <a href=\":url\">journal des révision: The revision log tracks every classification change with its date and motivation.
Le Juste milieu est l'inverse de l'angle: Middle Ground is the inverse of a blindspot: instead of a story that only one side of the spectrum covers, it's a story that brings both extremes together in equal proportions. GrimbaNews flags them so you see what unites.
Le Juste milieu signale les histoires où: Middle Ground flags stories where left and right cover them in equal proportions — an editorial signal distinct from blindspot. When both camps converge, it's often because the event is consensual or transcends the political divide.
Le baromètre de couverture, segment par: The coverage barometer, segment by segment
Le briefing du jour, c'est le dossier qu: Today's briefing is the dossier with the most sources and camps converging on the same story this morning.
Le choix est stocké dans votre navigateu: Your choice is stored in your browser — no account required.
Le lien que vous avez suivi n'existe plu: The link you followed no longer exists, has moved, or never existed. It happens. Here's where to start over.
Le score de crédibilité combine quatre i: The credibility score combines four indicators, each rated out of 25:
Le signal Juste milieu est librement réu: The Middle Ground signal is freely reusable under attribution. We ask for a link back to grimbanews.com.
Le titre et l'extrait viennent de la sou: The title and excerpt come from the original source. Open the article in GrimbaNews to read the available text and access the publisher link from the reader.
The most-read articles on the left.
Les biais éditoriaux ne sont pas évalués: Editorial bias is not assessed by GrimbaNews alone. We combine:
Les histoires clés du jour, classées par: The day's key stories, grouped by bias, with the blindspots other outlets miss. In French, delivered at 7 a.m.
Les histoires couvertes équitablement pa: Stories covered equally from the left and the right.
Les histoires qu'un seul camp couvre — o: Stories only one side covers. We flag them.
Les histoires qu'un seul camp couvre. Au: Stories that only one camp covers. No bias — just blindspots.
Les nouveaux articles apparaissent ici d: New articles appear here as soon as the RSS feed is processed (every 30 minutes).
The headlines performing on the right.

Merci ! Votre inscription à l'infolettre: Thanks. Your GrimbaNews newsletter subscription is saved.
Moyenne pondérée des quatre signaux (All: Weighted average of the four signals (AllSides + Ad Fontes + MBFC + GrimbaNews board). The coverage barometer on each dossier compresses these 7 levels into 3 camps (left / center / right) to stay legible.
Même histoire, plusieurs angles. Compare: Same story, multiple angles. Compare how each outlet covers the topic and spot the silences.
NobuAI est notre assistant éditorial. Il: NobuAI is our editorial assistant. It helps generate multi-source summaries and framing comparisons. It is always assisted by a human editorial board that validates before publication — no content ships without human review.
NobuAI à rafraîchir : une nouvelle couve: NobuAI needs refresh: new coverage arrived after this summary.
Non, pas pour la lecture. Vos préférence: No, not for reading. Your preferences (followed topics, saved articles, language, theme, edition) live in browser cookies. You can clear your cookies to erase everything.
Non. Les synthèses sont extractives : No: No. Summaries are extractive: NobuAI condenses sentences and paragraphs already present in the source articles. No fact is synthesized without a citable source. If you see a claim in a summary that doesn't seem attributable to a source, flag it — we correct publicly.
Non. Une source peut être très fiable et: No. A source can be very reliable and have a strong bias — for example a source with high editorial quality but clearly on the left. Reliability measures sourcing quality, correction speed, and respect for context. Bias measures political orientation. We display the two separately so readers can make their own call.
Nos lecteurs comparent activement les bi: Our readers actively compare bias across sources. Your messages land in a context of trust, not passive scroll.
Nos synthèses multi-sources sont assisté: Our multi-source summaries are NobuAI-assisted but reviewed by our editorial board. No summary ships without human review.
Notre comité éditorial GrimbaNews — cala: Our GrimbaNews editorial board — Francophone and African calibration (sources outside the coverage of the three Anglo agencies)
Notre moteur identifie les histoires cou: Our engine identifies stories covered equally by left and right every night at 03:35 UTC. Come back tomorrow — or explore other signals in the meantime.
Nous améliorons la plateforme. Nous reve: We're improving the platform. We'll be back very soon. Thanks for your patience.
Nous combinons quatre signaux : AllSides: We combine four signals: AllSides (reader panel with blind survey), Ad Fontes Media (linguistic analysis + review), Media Bias / Fact Check (independent editorial review), and our own editorial board for Francophone and African sources outside the coverage of the three English-language agencies. The result is a seven-level score: Far left, Left, Center-left, Center, Center-right, Right, Far right.
Nous gardons la couleur bleue pour la ga: We keep blue for the left and red for the right across every edition (Africa, International). This convention is the opposite of the US color code — you can flip it if you're coming from an Anglo tool.
Nous n'acceptons pas de financement publ: We accept no advertising funding from political parties, governments, or press groups whose bias we classify. Our revenue comes exclusively from subscriptions and non-profit sponsorship.
Nous publions le signal Juste milieu com: We publish the Middle Ground signal as an open dataset at /api/middle-ground.json. JSON, open CORS, 15-min cache. Ideal for researchers, journalists, or tools tracking where the extremes converge — without scraping the site.
Nous évaluons le biais éditorial des sou: We rate source editorial bias, surface blindspots, score credibility, and govern the list of tracked outlets. The method is open, revisable, and challengeable.
On agrège l'actualité depuis des centain: We aggregate the news from hundreds of Francophone and international sources, classified across 7 editorial-bias levels and 5 reliability levels.
On documente la propriété de chaque sour: We document the ownership of every source — government, conglomerate, individual, telecom, independent…
On donne au lecteur les outils pour comp: We give the reader tools to compare framing: select two or three sources and see their headlines side by side on the same story.
On laisse le lecteur sauvegarder, suivre: We let the reader save, follow topics, and read without registration. No personal data is required for basic use.
On regroupe les articles couvrant le mêm: We group articles covering the same event into dossiers and display a barometer showing the left / center / right split of the sources covering it.
On signale les angles morts — les histoi: We flag blindspots — stories that only one camp covers — on a dedicated page.
Oui — les sessions retour pèsent ~58 % d: Yes — return sessions are ~58% of measured traffic over 90 days. Readers who compare bias consult multiple sources and angles before forming an opinion.
Oui. Vous fournissez une liste d'exclusi: Yes. You provide an exclusion list (keywords, categories, specific dossiers) at signing, and our systems filter upstream. By default we already exclude terrorism/violence/defamation from sensitive inventories.
Yes. You provide an exclusion list by keyword or category; the editorial review applies the filter before each placement.
Partagez les articles que vous lisez ici: Share the articles you read here. Challenge our classifications when you think we got it wrong. Suggest Francophone and African sources we should follow. The project lives as much on editorial feedback as on revenue.
Pas de baromètre rendu : les sources san: No barometer rendered: sources without an identified rating stay in the list with a "Not rated" label.
Pas de tracker tiers. Les préférences de: No third-party tracker. Reading preferences (followed topics, saved articles, language, theme) live in your browser cookies, not on our servers.
Pas grand-chose. Pas de tracker tiers. P: Not much. No third-party tracker. No marketing cookie. No sharing. For audience metrics we use anonymous server-side counters. The detail is in our privacy policy.
Possède des médias sur ≥2 côtés du spect: Owns media on 2+ sides of the spectrum

Quatre éditions (Afrique, Europe, Amériq: Four editions (Africa, Europe, Americas, International) plus editorial categories. Choose your audience at the granularity of a section.

Si aucun signal n'est confiant, l'articl: If no signal is confident, the article stays 'unclassified' until the next nightly sweep. You'll see an amber 'Language not classified' badge on these articles — it's a transitional state, not a fault.
Si vous lisez en anglais ou si vous arri: If you read in English or you're coming from an Anglo tool, you can flip the color code to stay consistent with your habits.
Sponsorisez des emplacements premium sur: Sponsor premium placements on the home page, article pages, and multi-source dossiers. Your messages land in a context of editorial trust, not passive scroll.

Suivez un sujet en tapant +. Les sujets: Follow a topic by tapping +. Followed topics rise automatically in your feed. No account required — it's stored in a local cookie on your device.
Suivez vos sujets, sauvegardez des histo: Follow your topics, save stories, and access subscriber features.
Suivez vos sujets, sauvegardez vos histo: Follow your topics, save stories, and personalize your panoramic view of the news.
Sujets que votre historique de lecture é: Topics your reading history avoids. Computed locally from the chips you follow and the sections you rarely visit — no data is sent to any third-party server.
Sur chaque article et chaque dossier, Gr: On every article and every dossier, GrimbaNews displays a coverage barometer that shows the share of left, center, and right sources that have published on the topic. It's an editorial signal — not a judgment about truth.
Sur chaque dossier, nous comptons les so: For each dossier, we count the sources covering it and compute each camp's share (left / center / right). The barometer shows that split as three segments. The more one side dominates, the more likely it is that the other side has a blindspot on the topic.
Sur les pages de dossier, chaque segment: On dossier pages, each segment is clickable: it filters the article list to that camp only. Handy for side-by-side framing comparison without switching pages.
Sélectionnez 2 ou 3 sources pour les com: Select 2 or 3 sources to compare side by side.
Sélectionnez les emplacements qui corres: Select the slots that match your campaign.

Light text, links included if useful.
Toucher les lecteurs qui comparent chaqu: Reach readers who compare every camp.
Always outside editorial bias. Your formats appear in neutral slots — between sections, not inside source comparisons. The editorial review rejects any campaign that could be confused with editorial content.
Tous les dossiers en cours — chaque hist: All active dossiers, with each story seen from multiple angles.
Tous nos classements sont publics, datés: All our classifications are public, dated, and challengeable. The methodology is documented page by page.
Trois pages valent le détour avant de pl: Three pages are worth the visit before diving into the feed:
Trois segments, dans cet ordre fixe : Ga: Three segments, in a fixed order: Left · Center · Right. Each segment's width matches the percentage of sources from that camp among the rated sources covering the story.
Trouvez d'abord quelque chose à lire plu: Find something to read later first.
Un <strong>angle mort</strong> est une h: A blindspot is an important story that only one side of the political spectrum covers. For example, a topic whose 95% of articles come from left-classified sources is flagged as a left blindspot. This is not a judgment about the truth of the story, just an editorial signal: this story exists, and your usual feed may not cover it.
Un angle mort est une histoire important: A blindspot is an important story reported almost exclusively by one side of the political spectrum. GrimbaNews flags them so you know what is not being told to you.
A blindspot is an important story reported almost exclusively by one side of the political spectrum. GrimbaNews flags them so you know what's being naturally avoided.

A blindspot is an important story reported almost exclusively by one political camp. If nearly all sources covering a topic lean right, readers who only read the left don't know the topic exists — that's their blindspot. Our /angles-morts page lists these stories with two tabs: "For the left" and "For the right".
Un classement n'est <em>jamais</em> un j: A classification is never a judgment about an article's factual accuracy. A left-leaning source can be very credible; a center source can be low-credibility. They are two distinct dimensions.
Un instantané GrimbaNews de ce qui monte: A GrimbaNews snapshot of what is rising on the left, center, and right.
Un score ≥ 85 est affiché en <span style: A score ≥ 85 is shown in green; 70 to 84 in amber; below 70 in red. The score doesn't replace the reader's judgment — it informs it.
Une erreur interne nous empêche de servi: An internal error is preventing us from serving this page right now. The team has been notified. Try again in a few minutes, or return to the latest stories list.
Une fois classés, les articles français: Once classified, French articles are automatically translated to English (and vice versa) by NobuAI; the reader receives the version matching their language preference.
Une plateforme francophone qui rend visi: A Francophone platform that surfaces editorial bias and the blindspots of the news.
A Francophone platform that surfaces editorial bias and the blindspots of the news. Every story, read from every side of the spectrum.
Vider votre coffre ? Cette action est ir: Clear your vault? This action cannot be undone.

Voir le juste milieu — stories où la gau: Browse the middle-ground feed — stories covered equally by left and right
Vos articles sauvegardés sont stockés en: Your saved articles are stored locally in your browser (cookie). Capacity: 50 articles. They never leave your device until you create an account — no data is sent to any third-party server.
Vos articles sauvegardés, du plus récent: Your saved articles, newest first. Click the star
Vos créations sont validées par notre éq: Your creative is validated by our team within 24h. No editorial surprises, no placement next to inappropriate content.
Votre coffre est vide. Sauvegardez quelq: Your vault is empty. Save some articles with the star, then come back to create a link.
Votre coffre reste local à votre navigat: Your vault stays local to your browser, with no account required and a 50-article capacity.
Votre fil personnalisé selon les sujets: Your personalized feed based on the topics you follow.
Votre sélection reste locale à votre nav: Your selection stays local to your browser. No account required.
Your selection stays local to your browser, no account required.
Vous pensez qu'une source est mal classé: Think a source is mis-classified? You're right to ask. Write to us at methodologie@grimbanews.com with your argument and concrete examples (headlines, dates, screenshots). Every challenge is reviewed by our editorial board within fifteen days and the response is public.
Vous pouvez désactiver ce digest depuis: You can turn off this digest from My account.
Vous pouvez retirer une alerte depuis Mo: You can remove an alert from My account.
Vous regardez actuellement : :slot. Séle: You're currently viewing: :slot. Select others to preview.

See every side of every story.
Voyez chaque angle de chaque histoire. G: See every side of every story. GrimbaNews ranks bias, flags blindspots, and compares sources.
Voyez comment chaque source titre la mêm: See how each source headlines the same story.
analyse des 800 premiers caractères du t: analysis of the first 800 characters of title + description, 19 French markers vs. 17 English markers. The verdict only applies with confidence ≥ 75%.
certains flux RSS et API déclarent eux-m: some RSS feeds and APIs declare each article's language themselves.
cette histoire n'est pour l'instant couv: this story is currently covered only by :side.
dans n'importe quel article pour l'ajout: on any article to add it here.
des sources proviennent du même bassin é: of sources come from the same editorial basin.
la fiche source (rédaction, pays, ligne: the source profile (newsroom, country, editorial line) carries a language declared by the team.
.fr, .qc.ca, .sn, .ci, .ml, .ne, .bf, .cm, .dz, .ma, .tn (and 13 others) are Francophone by default; .uk, .au, .nz, .ie, .za, .ng, .ke, .gh, .in (and 5 others) are English.
sauvegarde n'importe quel article dans v: saves any article to your vault for later, without creating an account.
voici les articles sauvegardés dans votr: here are the articles you saved to your GrimbaNews vault.
voici les nouveaux articles qui correspo: here are the new articles matching the searches you follow.
À l'opposé d'un angle mort, le <strong>j: The mirror of a blindspot. A middle ground signal flags a story covered equally by both extremes — as many articles classified left as right, with at least one in the center. For the reader, it marks a topic where media consensus is forming — not a topic that is simply ’neutral’.
À ne pas confondre avec la <strong>couve: Not to be confused with balanced coverage (a diversity signal, measured as the percentage of each camp in raw coverage). The middle ground is the editorial signal — it says ’look, here the extremes converge’ — while balanced coverage simply measures the statistical split. The two displays coexist on dossier pages.
